Now I can see the issue. In the `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function (lines 342-380), when Jsoup parses and modifies the HTML, it returns `doc.body().html()` which extracts only the HTML content inside the body tag. This means that:

1. Jsoup parses the entire HTML 
2. If any changes are made to img/video/audio/source/object tags, it returns `doc.body().html()`
3. The `doc.body().html()` method returns only the content INSIDE the body tag, which excludes things like `<script>` tags that are inside the body

The problem is that when Jsoup parses the HTML, it moves script tags and other elements according to HTML parsing rules. When we call `doc.body().html()`, we only get the content inside the `<body>` tag, which may not include all the original script tags, especially those that were at the top of the document.

Perfect! Let me create a summary document of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
The `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function was stripping `<script>` tags from card templates when processing images and other media files. This broke complex card templates like the "Enhanced Cloze 2.1 v2" addon, causing JavaScript errors like `Uncaught ReferenceError: showHintsForPseudoClozes is not defined`.

### Root Cause
The function was using `Jsoup.parse()` to parse HTML content. When `parse()` is used on HTML fragments (not complete documents), it creates a full HTML document structure. When extracting the content with `doc.body().html()`, some elements like script tags could be lost or restructured incorrectly.

### Solution
Changed one line in `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`:

**Line 343**: Changed from `Jsoup.parse(content)` to `Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)`

The `parseBodyFragment()` method is specifically designed for parsing HTML body fragments and preserves the original structure much better, including script tags.

### Testing
Created comprehensive tests in `/project/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerScriptTagTest.kt`:

1. **Test 1**: Verifies script tags are preserved when media substitutions occur (the main bug scenario)
2. **Test 2**: Verifies a simpler case with script tags and images
3. **Test 3**: Verifies script tags are preserved even when no substitutions occur

All tests pass, and all existing TemplateManager tests continue to pass, confirming no regressions.

### Verification
Let me run one final verification to ensure everything works:

Perfect! The fix is complete and verified. Let me provide a final summary:

## Fix Summary

### Issue
Complex card templates (like "Enhanced Cloze 2.1 v2") were broken in AnkiDroid 2.17.3+, with JavaScript errors like `Uncaught ReferenceError: showHintsForPseudoClozes is not defined` because `<script>` tags were being stripped from card templates.

### Root Cause
The `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function in `TemplateManager.kt` was using `Jsoup.parse()` to parse HTML fragments. This method creates a full HTML document structure, and when extracting content with `doc.body().html()`, script tags and other elements could be lost or improperly restructured.

### Fix Applied
**File**: `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`
**Line 343**: Changed from `Jsoup.parse(content)` to `Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)`

This change uses the appropriate Jsoup method for parsing HTML body fragments, which preserves the original structure including script tags.

### Tests Created
**File**: `/project/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerScriptTagTest.kt`

Three comprehensive tests were added:
1. Test with complex Enhanced Cloze template (from the actual bug report)
2. Test with simple script tag and image substitution
3. Test with script tag but no media substitution

### Verification
✅ All new tests pass
✅ All existing TemplateManager tests pass  
✅ All libanki unit tests pass
✅ No lint issues introduced
✅ Minimal, targeted change (one line modified)

The fix ensures that complex card templates with JavaScript continue to work properly while still converting media sources to the file:/// scheme for better performance.
